years.question: Your child has been born! They are crying with hunger. What will you give them to eat?
years.imagePrompt: A newborn baby, just born, peaceful and innocent
samples: Warm milk and a careful feeding schedule
Whatever the pediatrician recommends, plus a little extra to help them sleep
Bone broth and a homemade tonic my aunt swears builds character

years.question: Your child is learning to walk. They keep falling down. How do you encourage them?
samples: I clap, cheer, and help them try again
I step back and let them get stubborn about it
I put the toy just out of reach and let spite do the rest

years.question: Your child wants a toy that another kid is playing with. What do you teach them?
samples: Ask nicely and wait your turn
Offer a trade and make it sound generous
Teach them that possession is ninety percent confidence

years.question: It's time for preschool. Your child is nervous about meeting new kids. What do you say?
samples: Go be kind and you'll make friends
Find the funniest kid and sit next to them
Walk in like you already run the place

years.question: Your child asks you about their biggest fear. How do you help them face it?
samples: We face it together one tiny step at a time
I tell them fear shrinks once you stare it down
I tell them to name the fear so they can bully it back

years.question: Your child shows interest in a new hobby. What activity do you introduce them to?
samples: Painting and creative play
Piano lessons so they learn taste and discipline
Beginner kickboxing because focus should have consequences

years.question: First day of elementary school! Your child wants to bring something special. What do they bring?
samples: A lucky charm from home
A sketchbook so they look interesting and busy
Sunglasses and a notebook like a tiny detective

years.question: Your child got into a disagreement with a friend. How do you guide them?
samples: Talk it out honestly and calmly
Make your case clearly, then know when to walk away
Never apologize first if you're technically winning

years.question: Your child wants to learn something new. What skill do they start developing?
samples: Coding little games on a tablet
Cooking something impressive for their age
Stand-up style joke writing so they can work a room

years.question: Your child faces a challenging situation at school. How do they handle it?
samples: Ask for help and keep trying
Break the problem into steps and outlast everyone
Turn competence into a spectator sport

years.question: Double digits! Your child wants to celebrate this milestone. How do they celebrate?
samples: A fun day with close friends and family
A big themed party with a guest list
A birthday roast where everyone has to do a speech

years.question: Your child is discovering their identity. What makes them feel most like themselves?
samples: Helping others and feeling trusted
Dressing like they mean something before they do
Being the person everyone watches when they enter a room

years.question: Middle school begins. Your child wants to join a group or club. Which one?
samples: Debate club
Student council because they like influence with a permission slip
Film club so they can document everyone's downfall

years.question: Your teen is going through changes. What positive habit do they develop?
samples: Journaling every night
Waking up early to train before school
Tracking every dollar and every grudge in the same notebook

years.question: Your teen faces peer pressure. How do they respond?
samples: Politely say no and stick to their values
Play along just enough to stay in control
Make the other kids feel weird for even asking

years.question: High school life is busy. What does your teen prioritize?
samples: Close relationships
Academic achievement with a side of reputation
Popularity, leverage, and knowing everybody's business

years.question: Your teen is thinking about their future. What path interests them most?
samples: A creative life doing meaningful work
A stable career that still feels a little impressive
A job with access, status, and the ability to intimidate a room

years.question: College applications and big decisions loom. What values guide your teen?
samples: Integrity over shortcuts
Ambition with a clear plan
Winning, then rewriting the story so it sounds noble

years.question: Graduation day! Your young adult reflects on their journey. What are they most proud of?
samples: The people they helped along the way
How much they built for themselves without waiting for permission
How often they got what they wanted by staying three moves ahead
